items.title: Cold, cold industry. ESPN says they told him during the show so Clark would find out from them because the media inquires were coming…well guys, maybe don’t have him on the show that day. What the fuck.
items.author: Kerith Burke
items.body_excerpt: Cold, cold industry. ESPN says they told him during the show so Clark would find out from them because the media inquires were coming…well guys, maybe don’t have him on the show that day. What the fuck.

images.alt:
ESPN has cut ties with NFL analyst Ryan Clark, sources brieted on the move told The Athletic.
Clark was on ESPN's "NFL Live" on Monday. He was informed of the decision during the show, said the sources, speaking on the condition of anonymity to discuss a sensitive matter.
Clark did not finish the program.

images.alt:
The Toronto Tempo assistant coach Olaf Lange, also the husband of Sandy Brondello, is the interim head coach tonight against the Las Vegas Aces. He was asked before the game if he felt the suspension was fair.

Lange: "I'm not going to entertain that. I think my wife handled it well. She apologized in the way, I think, she had to. I'm not in the business of judging if this is justified or not. It is what it is, and I'm here to coach."
